title: Sulfosalicylic acid to detect proteins in urine of pregnant women
author: Fernández Macedo, Cueva Rossel, et al.
description: Preeclampsia is one of the main causes of maternal mortality in the Department of Puno, this complication is a hypertensive disease of pregnancy, it is a public health problem worldwide, for this reason it is necessary to make a timely and preventive diagnosis.An alternative, for the confirmation of this disease, is the rapid detection of proteinuria, using sulfosalicylic acid, which, due to its predictive value, can be used in establishments that do not have personnel or laboratories to carry out clinical examinations.•It is an easy-to-use reagent that uses the urine of the pregnant woman.•Support for the diagnosis of preeclampsia in pregnancy•In addition, it is used to detect proteinuria in systemic diseases.

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: This research was of an analytical observational type. The participants were 84 pregnant women who had a diagnosis of Preeclampsia, referred from different health centers to the Manuel Núñez Butrón General Hospital in Puno

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: For sample processing, 24 h urine was collected from pregnant women.

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: In a test tube, 5 ml of urine was measured plus 3 drops of 3% sulfosalicylic acid, the color change was demonstrated on a black background, and it is interpreted: transparent (without proteinuria), cloudy (there is proteinuria), dense cloud with granules large and agglutination that can solidify greater than 500 mg. determines massive proteinuria of four crosses (++++), the degree of turbidity is directly proportional to the protein concentration according to the number of crosses present [15]. Fig. 2.

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: The data obtained were tabulated in the free software SPSS. Version 23; determining the sensitivity and specificity, for its validity and the

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: Positive Predictive Value and Negative Predictive Value, to see the safety of this diagnostic test, using the Reception Operative Characteristic Curve (ROC) [16]. resulting in a sensitivity of 78.3%, specificity of 47.4%. Fig. 1Fig. 1sensitivity and specificity of acetyl sulfasalicylic acid.Fig 1Fig. 2Turbidity classification. The different degrees of turbidity are appreciated by crosses, ranging from one to 4 crosses when urine reacts with sulfosalicylic acid.Fig 2
